name: Sierra de Pelardas por el Valle del Jiloca — circular desde Calamocha (BTT04)
region_intro: Mountain biking around Calamocha offers a diverse landscape for riders, situated in the middle of the extensive Jiloca plain. The Jiloca River flows through the region, creating a mix of flat areas and rolling hills. The terrain encompasses varied surfaces, providing engaging experiences across the Aragonese countryside. This region features significant elevation changes and a network of trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Calamocha The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Pelardas por el Valle del Jiloca — circular desde Calamocha (BTT04), a 32.0 miles (51.5 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 28 minutes to complete, exploring the Jiloca Valley. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Calamocha – Luco de Jiloca loop from Calamocha-Nueva, a difficult 46.8 miles (75.2 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views of the Aragonese countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Calamocha – El Poyo del Cid loop from Calamocha-Nueva, a 15.6 miles (25.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. Mountain biking around Calamocha is defined by the Jiloca plain, the Jiloca River, and varied surfaces across rolling hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Calamocha are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from a few reviews. More than 370 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Calamocha's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Calamocha?
A: Calamocha offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 45 routes to explore. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy to difficult.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Calamocha?
A: The region experiences cold winters and warm summers. While most rainfall occurs in winter, the varied climate means different seasons can offer distinct outdoor experiences. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant conditions for mountain biking.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Calamocha?
A: Yes, Calamocha has trails suitable for beginners. While the majority are moderate, there is at least one easy route available, allowing less experienced riders to enjoy the area's landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes in Calamocha for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Calamocha offers 12 difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calamocha – Luco de Jiloca loop from Calamocha-Nueva&lt;/a&gt;, which is 46.8 miles long with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find loop trails for mountain biking in Calamocha?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Calamocha are designed as loops. For instance, you can try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004877789/sierra-de-pelardas-por-el-valle-del-jiloca-circular-desde-calamocha-btt04" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Pelardas por el Valle del Jiloca — circular desde Calamocha (BTT04)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40003408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz loop from Calamocha-Nueva&lt;/a&gt;, both offering circular experiences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Calamocha's mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain around Calamocha is diverse, featuring a mix of flat areas and rolling hills across the extensive Jiloca plain. You'll encounter varied surfaces, including dry trails that provide good grip, offering engaging experiences through the Aragonese countryside.

Q: What are the elevation changes like on Calamocha's mountain bike trails?
A: Mountain bike trails in Calamocha can feature significant elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calamocha – Luco de Jiloca loop from Calamocha-Nueva&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1300 meters of ascent, providing challenging climbs and rewarding descents.

Q: What interesting landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Calamocha?
A: The area is rich in history. You can encounter landmarks such as the well-preserved &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge of Luco de Jiloca&lt;/a&gt; over the Jiloca River. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5058067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calamocha&lt;/a&gt; itself also has historical significance, with old churches and 16th-century manor houses.

Q: Are there any unique natural features to explore near Calamocha?
A: Yes, a notable natural feature nearby is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento&lt;/a&gt;, which showcases a unique karst relief with a waterfall and small caves formed by continuously falling spring water. The Jiloca River also adds riparian landscapes to the biking experience.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Calamocha?
A: The mountain bike trails in Calamocha are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Over 370 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and engaging experiences.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Calamocha?
A: While most trails are moderate to difficult, there are options for families. Look for the 'easy' graded routes, which offer a gentler introduction to mountain biking in the region's scenic environment.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with good viewpoints in Calamocha?
A: Many routes in the Calamocha area offer panoramic views of the Aragonese countryside. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calamocha – Luco de Jiloca loop from Calamocha-Nueva&lt;/a&gt;, with its significant elevation gain, provide excellent opportunities to enjoy expansive vistas.

Q: Can I find parking near the mountain bike trails in Calamocha?
A: As Calamocha is a town and a hub for many routes, you can typically find parking within or near the town center, providing convenient access to the start points of various trails.
tips:
Ollala is a town belonging to the municipality of Calamocha and the Jiloca region that has 44 inhabitants and one of the best preserved juniper forests in Aragon that we will cross by bike if we follow the Sierra de Pelarda route from the Jiloca BTT center.
It is worth noting the Mudejar-style tower known as the Tower of the old church.
Near the town of Nueros we find these beautiful vitas of the surrounding crops while we go through one of the BTT routes in the center of Jiloca, the Sierra de Pelarda.

waypoints.name: Mudéjar Tower of the Old Church of Olalla
